skill.name: linkedin-easy-apply
skill.description: Automate LinkedIn Easy Apply searches and applications with Puppeteer/Chromium, a verified resume PDF, remote/job-title filtering, stateful daily reruns, and conservative answer guardrails.
skill.summary: Automate LinkedIn job applications using browser automation with resume uploads, job filtering, and stateful reruns to avoid duplicate submissions.
skill.author: ralyodio

security_audit.summary: Skill is a documentation/guide template for building LinkedIn Easy Apply automation. Static scanner flagged 48 potential issues but most are false positives from code examples in documentation. Confirmed legitimate browser automation patterns. Primary concerns are (1) LinkedIn Terms of Service violations for automated job applications, (2) pattern detection for Puppeteer/Chrome flags including --disable-setuid-sandbox which is a standard Chrome launch option. Skill explicitly avoids credential storage and includes conservative answer guardrails.

high_findings.title: LinkedIn Terms of Service Violation Risk
high_findings.description: Automated job application submissions likely violate LinkedIn's Terms of Service. Users risk account suspension or legal action from LinkedIn. This is a business/legal risk rather than technical security risk.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Skill is explicitly designed to automate LinkedIn Easy Apply, which LinkedIn prohibits. Line 42 mentions stopping for CAPTCHA but does not address ToS compliance.

medium_findings.title: Chrome Security Flags Misidentified
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged --disable-setuid-sandbox as UID/GID manipulation. This is a standard Chrome launch flag for containerized environments, not malicious UID manipulation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The --disable-setuid-sandbox flag is documented Chromium behavior for running in Docker containers. No actual UID/GID changes occur.

medium_findings.title: Code Examples Flagged as External Commands
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected backtick execution patterns in code examples. These are documentation examples, not actual executions in the skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All external command detections are in code blocks showing example Puppeteer/JavaScript patterns. Skill is a documentation template, not executable code.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for Configuration
low_findings.description: Skill uses environment variables for configuration (RESUME_PDF, CHROME_PROFILE, STATE_DIR). This is standard configuration pattern, not credential exposure.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variables are documented configuration values, not secrets. Line 25 explicitly states skill contains no credentials.

content.user_title: Automate LinkedIn Easy Apply job applications with Puppeteer
content.value_statement: Job seekers spend hours manually applying to LinkedIn Easy Apply positions. This skill enables AI agents to automate the search, filtering, resume upload, and application process while respecting conservative answer guardrails and maintaining state across reruns.
actual_capabilities: Search LinkedIn jobs with Easy Apply filter using configurable keywords and location constraints
Launch Puppeteer with Chromium to interact with LinkedIn job pages reliably
Upload verified resume PDF to job application forms
Apply conservative answer rules based on verified resume facts only
Maintain state across daily reruns to avoid duplicate applications
Skip unknown required fields, compensation questions, and custom essays
limitations: Cannot handle CAPTCHA, MFA, or identity verification challenges
May violate LinkedIn Terms of Service leading to account restrictions
Requires manual operator login once to establish browser profile
Cannot fabricate applicant facts or answer questions without verified information

use_cases.title: Job Search Automation for Busy Professionals
use_cases.description: A professional who has limited time can use this skill to automate applying to matching LinkedIn Easy Apply positions while they focus on interview preparation.
use_cases.target_user: Job seekers with limited time for job applications

use_cases.title: Bulk Applications for Specific Role Types
use_cases.description: A developer targeting AI, LLM, or full-stack engineer positions can configure keyword filters and let the automation handle volume applications.
use_cases.target_user: Software engineers seeking multiple similar positions

use_cases.title: Daily Job Monitoring Workflow
use_cases.description: A recruiter or job hopper can schedule daily reruns with state persistence to catch new postings without reapplying to already-submitted positions.
use_cases.target_user: Active job seekers conducting ongoing job searches

prompt_templates.title: Basic LinkedIn Easy Apply Run
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the LinkedIn Easy Apply skill with these settings: search for [KEYWORD] jobs in [LOCATION], upload my resume at [PATH], and apply to up to 10 jobs matching my criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Remote-Only Search with State
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute the LinkedIn Easy Apply workflow with REMOTE_ONLY=1, SEARCHES='Claude|OpenAI|AI engineer', and DRY_RUN=0. Track state to /tmp/linkedin-easyapply-daily/ and report results.
prompt_templates.scenario: Remote-only search for AI-related positions with state tracking

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Day Job Search Campaign
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a LinkedIn Easy Apply campaign targeting [ROLE_TYPE] positions. Configure MAX_SCAN=80, MAX_APPLY=10, and ensure state persists between runs. Skip any job requiring salary disclosure.
prompt_templates.scenario: Campaign setup with specific constraints and state management

prompt_templates.title: Review Before Submission
prompt_templates.prompt: Run LinkedIn Easy Apply with DRY_RUN=1 first to review which jobs would be matched. After approval, set DRY_RUN=0 to submit applications. Include detailed logging of skipped jobs with reasons.
prompt_templates.scenario: Dry run review before actual application submission

output_examples.input: Run LinkedIn Easy Apply with SEARCHES='software engineer' and LOCATION='United States'
output_examples.output:
Submitted: Senior Software Engineer - Acme Corp - https://linkedin.com/jobs/view/123
Submitted: Full Stack Engineer - TechStart Inc - https://linkedin.com/jobs/view/456
Skipped: Software Engineer Intern - Budget Co - https://linkedin.com/jobs/view/789 - requires salary expectation
State: /tmp/linkedin-easyapply-daily/state.json
Log: /tmp/linkedin-easyapply-daily/results.jsonl

output_examples.input: Execute with DRY_RUN=1 to preview matches
output_examples.output:
Preview Mode - Would apply to:
- AI Engineer - OpenAI - https://linkedin.com/jobs/view/111
- LLM Specialist - Anthropic - https://linkedin.com/jobs/view/222
Would skip:
- Junior Developer - Unknown Startup - missing required field (custom essay)
Review these before setting DRY_RUN=0 to submit.

best_practices: Always set DRY_RUN=1 first to preview which jobs will be matched before actual submission
Manually log into LinkedIn once using a persistent browser profile to avoid repeated authentication
Use conservative answer rules and skip any job requiring information not verified on your resume
anti_patterns: Do not store LinkedIn credentials in environment variables or scripts - operators should log in manually
Do not skip CAPTCHA or security prompts - stop execution when these appear to protect your account
Do not answer compensation questions or custom essays without explicit operator approval

faq.question: Does this violate LinkedIn Terms of Service?
faq.answer: Yes, automated job applications may violate LinkedIn's Terms of Service. Use this skill at your own risk and consider whether your use case is acceptable to LinkedIn.

faq.question: How do I avoid getting my LinkedIn account suspended?
faq.answer: Use conservative settings, stop for CAPTCHAs, do not exceed reasonable application volumes, and ensure your browser profile appears natural to LinkedIn.

faq.question: Can this work with LinkedIn requiring MFA?
faq.answer: No. MFA and identity verification challenges require manual human intervention. The skill should stop when these prompts appear.

faq.question: How do I configure the job search keywords?
faq.answer: Set the SEARCHES environment variable with pipe-separated keywords like SEARCHES='Claude|OpenAI|AI engineer|full stack' to match your target roles.

faq.question: Why are my applications being skipped?
faq.answer: The skill skips applications when required fields cannot be answered from verified facts, when compensation questions are present, or when the job requires custom essays.

faq.question: How does state tracking prevent duplicate applications?
faq.answer: State is saved to state.json with applied job IDs. Daily reruns check this file and skip already-submitted positions to avoid duplicates.
